description: Add a new attribute to a metadata object (Catalog, Document, register, etc.) with default properties, persisted to disk. Use to extend an object's data model; the parentFqn TYPE token may be English or Russian. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('add_metadata_attribute').

expectedNotExists.description: Optional stale-intent guard (default false): assert no such attribute exists yet; if it does, fail with a re-read steer instead of the generic duplicate error

language.description: Optional language code for the synonym, e.g. 'ru'/'en' (default: config default)

parentFqn.description: FQN of the parent object, e.g. 'Catalog.Products' (TYPE token may be en/ru; object part is the programmatic Name, not the synonym)

synonym.description: Optional display name; written for 'language' or the config default language

name: add_metadata_attribute

attributeName.description: Programmatic name of the added attribute

language.description: Language code the synonym was written for

parentFqn.description: Normalized FQN of the parent object

persisted.description: Whether the change was saved to disk

synonym.description: Display name written, when a synonym was provided

description: Clean EDT project and trigger full revalidation. Refreshes files from disk, clears all validation markers, and waits for EDT to complete revalidation.

projectName.description: Name of the project to clean (optional, cleans all EDT projects if not specified)

name: clean_project

projects.description: Names of the projects that were cleaned

projectsCleaned.description: Number of projects that were cleaned

description: Create a new top-level metadata object (Catalog, Document, register, etc.) with EDT default content, the same as the 'New' wizard. Use when adding an object to a configuration; supported types: Catalog, Document, InformationRegister, AccumulationRegister, Enum, CommonModule, Report, DataProcessor (Russian type names also accepted). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('create_metadata_object').

comment.description: Optional comment for the new object.

expectedNotExists.description: Optional stale-intent guard (default false): assert the object does not yet exist for a sharper precondition error. A real duplicate is always rejected anyway.

language.description: Optional language code for the synonym (e.g. 'ru', 'en'); defaults to config default language.

metadataType.description: Type to create: 'Catalog', 'Document', 'InformationRegister', 'AccumulationRegister', 'Enum', 'CommonModule', 'Report', 'DataProcessor' (Russian names also accepted).

name.description: Name for the new object; must be a valid 1C identifier (e.g. 'Products').

synonym.description: Optional display name; written for the config default language unless 'language' is set.

name: create_metadata_object

fqn.description: FQN of the created object, e.g. 'Catalog.Products'

language.description: Language code the synonym was written for

metadataType.description: Canonical metadata type of the created object

name.description: Programmatic name of the created object

persisted.description: Whether the change was saved to disk

synonym.description: Display name written, when a synonym was provided

description: Start an EDT debug session: either an existing config by launchConfigurationName (runtime client OR Attach, the latter needed to debug server-side code), or a runtime-client config matched by projectName + applicationId. If that config is already running it short-circuits with alreadyRunning:true (terminate_launch first to force a restart). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('debug_launch').

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications; required in the projectName+applicationId mode.

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact name of an EDT debug launch config (runtime client or Attach); skips projectName/applicationId.

projectName.description: EDT project name; required unless launchConfigurationName is given.

updateBeforeLaunch.description: Update the database before launching. Default true; ignored for Attach.

name: debug_launch

alreadyRunning.description: True if a matching session was already alive; re-launch skipped

applicationId.description: Application id of the launched configuration

attach.description: True if this is an Attach (server-side debug) configuration

launchConfiguration.description: Name of the launched/running launch configuration

mode.description: Launch mode of the session (e.g. debug, run)

project.description: EDT project name associated with the launch

description: Report active debug launches: applicationId (real or synthetic 'attach:<name>'), launch configuration name/type, mode (debug/run), whether the target is currently suspended, thread count, and the line of the top suspended frame. Optionally filter by applicationId.
name: debug_status

count.description: Number of active debug launches returned

launches.description: Active debug launches with state and frame info

description: Launch YAXUnit tests in DEBUG mode so breakpoints fire, then call wait_for_break to inspect; use when you need to step through test-driven code, not just get a pass/fail report (use run_yaxunit_tests for that). Requires an existing 1C launch configuration and YAXUnit installed in the infobase. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('debug_yaxunit_tests').

applicationId.description: Application id from get_applications (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted).

extensions.description: Comma-separated extension names to filter tests by extension.

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact runtime-client launch config name (preferred; from list_configurations).

modules.description: Comma-separated module names to filter tests.

projectName.description: EDT project name (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted).

tests.description: Comma-separated test names as Module.Method (recommended: pin to one test for a predictable cycle).

updateBeforeLaunch.description: Default true: terminate any live client and run a silent DB update first so no modal 'Update database?' dialog blocks the call; false keeps legacy delegate behaviour.

name: debug_yaxunit_tests

applicationId.description: Effective application id of the launched config

junitXml.description: Full path to the junit.xml report file

launched.description: Whether the debug launch was queued

nextStep.description: Hint for the next tool call in the debug cycle

preLaunch.description: Summary of pre-launch terminations and DB update

projectName.description: Effective EDT project name of the launched config

reportDir.description: Directory where junit.xml will be written

description: Delete a metadata object or attribute, cascading the cleanup of all references in BSL code, forms, and other metadata. Use the two-phase workflow: call without confirm to preview affected references, review, then call with confirm=true to apply (deletion is hard to reverse). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('delete_metadata_object').

confirm.description: true = execute the deletion; default false = preview only.

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object to delete, e.g. 'Catalog.Products' or 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount' (Russian type token also accepted; name is the programmatic Name, not the synonym).

name: delete_metadata_object

items.description: Metadata items the deletion would remove (preview)

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object targeted for deletion

description: Evaluate a BSL expression in the context of a suspended stack frame. Pass frameRef from wait_for_break and the expression text. WARNING: this executes arbitrary BSL code in the running 1C application.
name: evaluate_expression

fullLength.description: Full length of value before truncation

truncated.description: True when value was truncated to the max length

type.description: BSL reference type name of the evaluated value

value.description: String representation of the evaluated value (may be truncated)

description: Export an EDT configuration project to XML files (EDT menu: Export -> Configuration to XML Files). Equivalent of 1C platform DumpConfigToFiles.

outputPath.description: Filesystem path of the output directory for the XML files (required)

name: export_configuration_to_xml

description: Find every place a metadata object is used: BSL code modules (with line numbers), other metadata, forms, roles, subsystems, etc. Pass the object FQN; the type token may be English or Russian (e.g. 'Catalog.Products' or its Russian spelling). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('find_references').

limit.description: Result-size hint (default 100, max 500); caps the overall reference count (at limit*10), not per category.

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object to search for, e.g. 'Catalog.Products' (type token may be English or Russian) (required)

name: find_references

description: Generate translation strings (.lstr/.trans/.dict) for a configuration project: scans translatable features and writes the resulting keys into the project's storages (EDT menu Translation -> Generate translation strings). Run on the configuration project (V8ConfigurationNature), not a dictionary storage project; requires LanguageTool installed in EDT. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('generate_translation_strings').

collectModelType.description: Model mode: ANY | NONE | COMPUTED_ONLY | UNKNOWN_ONLY | TAGS_ONLY. Default: ANY.

fillUpType.description: Pre-fill source: NOT_FILLUP | FROM_SOURCE_LANGUAGE | FROM_PROVIDER. Default: NOT_FILLUP.

projectName.description: Configuration project name (V8ConfigurationNature), not a dictionary storage project. Required.

providerId.description: Translation provider ID; required only when fillUpType=FROM_PROVIDER (see get_translation_project_info).

storageId.description: Storage ID to write keys into (see get_translation_project_info). Default: "edit:default".

targetLanguages.description: Target language codes to generate, e.g. ["en"]. Required.

name: generate_translation_strings

description: Get list of applications (infobases) for a project. Returns application ID, name, type, and update state. Application ID is required for update_database and debug_launch tools.
name: get_applications

applications.description: Applications with id, name, type and update state

defaultApplicationId.description: Id of the project's default application

message.description: Informational message when no applications are found

project.description: EDT project name the applications belong to

description: Get bookmarks from the workspace. Returns bookmark message, file path, and line number.
name: get_bookmarks

description: Get detailed description of an EDT check by its ID. Returns markdown content with check explanation, examples, and how to fix. Requires a configured check-descriptions folder (MCP preferences); without it the tool returns a configuration error.
name: get_check_description

description: Get 1C:Enterprise configuration properties (name, synonym, comment, script variant, compatibility mode, etc.)

projectName.description: Project name (optional, if not specified returns first configuration project)

name: get_configuration_properties

description: Get code-completion proposals at a 1-based line/column in a BSL module - the members, globals and variables valid at that caret (e.g. after a '.'). May return a 'not ready' error while the editor loads; just retry. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_content_assist').

contains.description: Keep proposals whose display string contains any of these substrings (comma-separated, e.g. 'Insert,Add')

extendedDocumentation.description: Include full documentation per proposal (default: false)

limit.description: Max proposals to return (default: from preferences, max 1000)

modulePath.description: BSL module path under src (e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl'); alias: filePath

offset.description: Skip first N matching proposals for pagination (default: 0)

name: get_content_assist

file.description: Workspace-relative path of the module the proposals are for

filteredOut.description: Proposals removed by the contains filter

returnedProposals.description: Count of proposals returned in this page

totalProposals.description: Total proposals offered before any filter

description: Returns the running 1C:EDT version as a plain version string. Returns "Unknown" when the version cannot be determined.
name: get_edt_version

description: Return a YAML snapshot of a form's calculated WYSIWYG layout (bounds, element types, display properties) as text; use it to inspect or compare what a form actually renders. Requires EDT launched with -DnativeFormBufferedLayoutRender=true, else the result is blank (missing flag, not a bad call). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_form_layout_snapshot').

formPath.description: Form FQN (e.g. 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm' or 'CommonForm.MyForm'); if omitted, uses the active form editor.

mode.description: Output mode: 'compact' (default, visible elements only) or 'full' (all nodes/properties)

projectName.description: EDT project name. Required when formPath is specified.

refresh.description: Force WYSIWYG refresh before snapshot (default: true)

name: get_form_layout_snapshot

description: Capture a PNG screenshot of a form's WYSIWYG editor; pass formPath to open the form automatically or omit it to shoot the active editor. Requires EDT launched with -DnativeFormBufferedLayoutRender=true, else the image is blank (missing flag, not a bad call). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_form_screenshot').

formPath.description: Form FQN (e.g. 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm' or 'CommonForm.MyForm'); if omitted, captures the active form editor.

projectName.description: EDT project name. Required when formPath is specified.

refresh.description: Force WYSIWYG refresh before capture (default: false)

name: get_form_screenshot

description: Read the editable model tree of a managed form (nested items, attributes, commands) with each item's programmatic name and integer id. Use to get the addressing ids that form-write tools need, NOT the rendered WYSIWYG layout (use get_form_layout_snapshot for bounds). Reads the BM model directly; does not open the form editor. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_form_structure').

formPath.description: Form FQN: 'MetadataType.ObjectName.Forms.FormName' or 'CommonForm.FormName' (e.g. 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm'). Names are the programmatic Name, not the synonym.

language.description: Language code for titles, e.g. 'en'/'ru'. Defaults to the configuration language.

name: get_form_structure

description: Get detailed properties of one or more 1C metadata objects (basic info by default, or every reflected section with 'full: true'). Use it after get_metadata_objects to inspect a known object's attributes/forms/commands; in full mode each section is capped so request fewer FQNs to keep the response small. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_metadata_details').

full.description: All reflected properties (true) or only key info (false). Default: false

language.description: Synonym language code, e.g. 'en'/'ru' (default: configuration default)

objectFqns.description: Required. FQNs as Type.Name, e.g. ['Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder']; Russian type tokens also work (e.g. 'Справочник.Products').

name: get_metadata_details

description: Get a flat list of 1C configuration metadata objects (Name, Synonym, Comment, Type, ObjectModule, ManagerModule) as a Markdown table. Use it to discover what objects exist; filter by metadataType and/or nameFilter (Name only). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_metadata_objects').

language.description: Synonym language code, e.g. 'en'/'ru' (default: configuration default)

metadataType.description: Type filter (case-insensitive), default 'all'. One of: all, documents, catalogs, informationRegisters, accumulationRegisters, commonModules, enums, constants, reports, dataProcessors, exchangePlans, businessProcesses, tasks, commonAttributes, eventSubscriptions, scheduledJobs

nameFilter.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against Name only (not Synonym)

name: get_metadata_objects

description: Find a BSL method's call hierarchy: who calls it (callers, default) or what it calls (callees), via semantic AST analysis that resolves ru/en spellings (unlike literal search_in_code). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_method_call_hierarchy').
name: get_method_call_hierarchy

description: Get structure of a BSL module: all procedures/functions with signatures, line numbers, regions, execution context (&AtServer, &AtClient), export flag, and parameters. responseFormat=concise (default) returns a leaner methods table (drops the verbose Parameters and Description columns; keeps type, name, export, context, lines, region); responseFormat=detailed returns the full table with signatures and doc-comments. Use detailed when you need parameter lists or descriptions.

includeComments.description: Include documentation comments for methods. Default: false

includeVariables.description: Include module-level variable declarations. Default: false

responseFormat.description: Output verbosity. concise (default) = leaner methods table (drops Parameters and Description columns); detailed = full table with signatures and doc-comments.

name: get_module_structure

description: Get metadata objects filtered by tags. Returns objects that have any of the specified tags, including tag descriptions and object FQNs.

limit.description: Maximum number of objects to return per tag. Default: 100

tags.description: Array of tag names to filter by (e.g. ['Important', 'NeedsReview']). Returns objects that have ANY of these tags. Required.

name: get_objects_by_tags

description: Look up 1C:Enterprise platform documentation for built-in types (ValueTable, Array, Structure) and global built-in functions, including their methods, properties, constructors and events. Use when you need the exact platform API signature rather than configuration metadata. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_platform_documentation').

memberName.description: Filter members by name, partial match (e.g. 'Add').

projectName.description: EDT project name to pin the platform version. Optional.

responseFormat.description: 'concise' (default) = leaner: headers + member names only; 'detailed' = full member signatures, parameters, types and flags.

typeName.description: Type or symbol name, English or Russian (e.g. 'ValueTable').

name: get_platform_documentation

description: Get problem summary with counts grouped by project and EDT severity level (ERRORS, BLOCKER, CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, TRIVIAL).

projectName.description: Name of the project (optional, all projects if not specified)

name: get_problem_summary

description: Get profiling (performance measurement) results after a debug session: per-module, per-line call count, timing and percentage. Also reports whether profiling is currently active. Run start_profiling + the test (then stop_profiling) first. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_profiling_results').

applicationId.description: Debug session id; when set, reports active state for that session

minFrequency.description: Only include lines called at least N times (default: 1)

moduleFilter.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on module name

responseFormat.description: concise (default) = leaner per-line rows (line, calls, pct only); detailed = full rows incl. code text, method signature and dur/pureDur timing

name: get_profiling_results

count.description: Number of profiling result sets returned

message.description: Informational note when no results are available

results.description: Per-result sets: name, totalDurability, moduleCount and modules map of lines

description: List EDT configuration problems (validation markers) with optional project / severity / check-id / object filters. Each row carries the check code, message, object location and severity; BSL-module problems also expose a structural locator (Module path + Line) you can feed straight into read_module_source or set_breakpoint. Object FQN filters accept English or Russian type names (e.g. 'Catalog.Products'). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_project_errors').

checkId.description: Filter by check-id substring; matches the symbolic id (e.g. 'ql-temp-table-index') or short UID (e.g. 'SU23') (optional)

objects.description: Filter by object FQNs, e.g. ['Catalog.Products']; English or Russian type names accepted (optional)

projectName.description: Filter by EDT project name; omit to scan all projects (optional)

responseFormat.description: Output verbosity (optional): concise (default) = leaner table without the secondary 'Has docs' column; detailed = full table including 'Has docs'

name: get_project_errors

description: Self-diagnosis snapshot of the running MCP server: listening port, MCP protocol version, plugin version, EDT version, enabled/total tool counts, the plainTextMode and checksFolderConfigured preference flags, the two form-render JVM flags (nativeFormBufferedLayoutRender / nativeFormLayoutRender), and whether authentication is enabled. Use it to explain a blank form screenshot or a plain-text JSON response. Never returns the auth token value or the checks folder path, only booleans.
name: get_server_status

checksFolderConfigured.description: Whether a checks folder path is configured

formRenderFlags.description: Form-render JVM flag states keyed by flag name

plainTextMode.description: Whether JSON responses are forced to plain text

port.description: TCP port the MCP server listens on

protocolVersion.description: MCP protocol version implemented by the server

running.description: Whether the MCP server is currently running

description: Get one 1C subsystem's content: properties, its metadata objects (Type/Name/Synonym/FQN) and child subsystems, identified by FQN (e.g. 'Subsystem.Sales.Subsystem.Orders'). By default lists only this subsystem's objects; set recursive=true to fold in nested ones. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_subsystem_content').

language.description: Synonym language code, e.g. 'en'/'ru' (default: configuration default)

recursive.description: Also include objects from nested subsystems (default: false)

name: get_subsystem_content

description: Get type/hover info about a symbol at a position in a BSL module. Returns inferred types, signatures, and documentation.

modulePath.description: BSL module path from src/, e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl' (canonical; alias: filePath)

name: get_symbol_info

description: Get list of all tags defined in the project. Tags are user-defined labels for organizing metadata objects. Returns tag name, color, description, and number of assigned objects.
name: get_tags

description: Get tasks (TODO, FIXME, etc.) from the workspace. Returns task message, file path, line number, and priority.
name: get_tasks

description: Get the full on-demand how-to for a tool: its description, every parameter (type, required, allowed values) and extended examples/preconditions kept OUT of the always-loaded tool list to save context. Pass the exact tool name from tools/list. Also available as the resource guide://<toolName>.

toolName.description: The exact name of the tool to document, as it appears in tools/list (e.g. 'read_module_source').

name: get_tool_guide

description: Return LanguageTool metadata for a project: the translation storages declared on it and the available translation provider IDs. Use it to check whether a dictionary storage is attached before translating; an empty storages list means none is attached yet (set up manually in EDT). Requires EDT with LanguageTool installed. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('get_translation_project_info').
name: get_translation_project_info

description: Read variables from a stack frame of a suspended debug thread. Pass frameRef from wait_for_break (preferred) or threadId+frameIndex. Use expandPath to drill into nested structures (dot-separated).

expandPath.description: Dot-separated path to expand a nested variable

frameRef.description: Stable frame reference returned from wait_for_break

name: get_variables

variables.description: Variables in the frame or expanded node, with name/value/type

description: Go to the definition of a symbol (the inverse of find_references): a qualified method 'ModuleName.MethodName', a bare 'MethodName' (also pass modulePath), or a metadata FQN like 'Catalog.Products'. A bare method name requires modulePath. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('go_to_definition').

modulePath.description: Module path from src/, e.g. 'CommonModules/My/Module.bsl'; required for a bare method name

name: go_to_definition

description: Import a configuration from a directory of XML files into a NEW EDT project (EDT menu: Import); the reverse of export_configuration_to_xml. The projectName must not already exist in the workspace. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('import_configuration_from_xml').

importPath.description: Path of the source directory of XML files.

projectName.description: Name of the NEW EDT project to create (must not already exist).

projectNature.description: Optional EDT nature ID, e.g. 'com._1c.g5.v8.dt.core.V8ConfigurationNature'; empty = auto-detect.

xmlVersion.description: Optional XML format version, e.g. '8.3.20'; empty = auto-detect.

name: import_configuration_from_xml

description: List active line breakpoints. Optionally filter by projectName.
name: list_breakpoints

description: List EDT launch configurations (runtime client + Attach + other 1C types) with their running state. This is the discovery step before debug_launch / run_yaxunit_tests / debug_yaxunit_tests / update_database: use the returned 'name' as their launchConfigurationName. Use type='attach' for server-side debug setups, type='client' for 1C:Enterprise client configs, or type='all' (default). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('list_configurations').

type.description: Filter by config kind; default 'all'. e.g. 'attach' for server-side debug.

name: list_configurations

configurations.description: Matching launch configurations with their state.

description: List BSL modules in an EDT project as a table (module path, module type, parent type, parent name). Use it to discover module paths before reading or editing code; filter by metadataType, objectName or nameFilter. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('list_modules').

metadataType.description: Type filter, default 'all'. One of: all, documents, catalogs, commonModules, informationRegisters, accumulationRegisters, reports, dataProcessors, exchangePlans, businessProcesses, tasks, constants, commonCommands, commonForms, webServices, httpServices

nameFilter.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against the module path

objectName.description: Programmatic Name of one object to scope to, e.g. 'Products' (case-insensitive)

name: list_modules

description: List all workspace projects with properties (name, path, type, natures)
name: list_projects

description: List 1C subsystems of a configuration as a flat table (FQN, Synonym, Comment, InCommandInterface, content count, children count). Walks the whole tree by default (recursive=true); use get_subsystem_content for one subsystem's objects. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('list_subsystems').

language.description: Synonym language code, e.g. 'en'/'ru' (default: configuration default)

nameFilter.description: Case-insensitive partial match on Name only (not Synonym)

name: list_subsystems

description: Read a specific procedure/function from a BSL module by name. Returns source code with metadata. Lists available methods if not found.
name: read_method_source

description: Read BSL module source code from an EDT project, whole file or a line range. Returns YAML frontmatter (including a contentHash revision token to round-trip into write_module_source's expectedHash) followed by clean source in a fenced bsl block. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('read_module_source').

endLine.description: Last line, 1-based inclusive; omit to read to the end.

startLine.description: First line, 1-based inclusive; omit to read from the start.

name: read_module_source

description: Remove a 1C BSL line breakpoint. Either pass breakpointId (returned from set_breakpoint) or projectName+module+lineNumber to look it up by coordinates.

lineNumber.description: 1-based line number (when looking up by coordinates)

modulePath.description: EDT module path or absolute path (when looking up by coordinates)

projectName.description: EDT project name (when looking up by coordinates)

name: remove_breakpoint

removed.description: True if a matching breakpoint was found and removed

description: Rename a metadata object or attribute, cascading the change across all references in BSL code, forms, and other metadata. Use the two-phase workflow: call without confirm for an indexed preview of every change point, review it, then call again with confirm=true to apply. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('rename_metadata_object').

confirm.description: true = apply the rename; default false = preview only.

disableIndices.description: Comma-separated preview '#' indices of OPTIONAL change points to skip, e.g. '2,3,5'.

maxResults.description: Max change points shown in the preview (default 20; 0 = no limit).

newName.description: New programmatic Name for the object.

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object to rename, e.g. 'Catalog.Products' or 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount' (Russian type names also accepted).

name: rename_metadata_object

description: Resume a suspended debug thread or all threads of a debug target. Pass threadId (from wait_for_break) or applicationId. With no arguments, resumes the single active debug launch if exactly one exists.

applicationId.description: Application id (real or 'attach:<configName>' — resumes all threads of this target)

name: resume

applicationId.description: Application id of the resumed debug target

autoResolved.description: Whether the lone active launch was auto-resolved

resumed.description: Whether the thread or target was resumed

description: Revalidate EDT project or specific objects. If objects array is empty or missing, revalidates entire project. FQN examples: 'Document.SalesOrder', 'Catalog.Products', 'CommonModule.Common'. Russian type names are also supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПриходнаяНакладная', 'Справочник.Номенклатура').

objects.description: FQNs to revalidate (e.g. ['Document.SalesOrder']). Russian type names supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПродажаТоваров'). Empty array = full project revalidation

name: revalidate_objects

description: Run YAXUnit tests for a 1C:Enterprise project and return a JUnit Markdown report. Polls for up to `timeout` seconds, then returns the report or **Pending** (call again with identical arguments to keep waiting; the launch is not terminated). Requires an existing runtime-client launch configuration and the YAXUnit extension installed in the infobase. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('run_yaxunit_tests').

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted).

extensions.description: Comma-separated extension names to filter tests.

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact runtime-client launch config name (preferred; from list_configurations).

modules.description: Comma-separated module names to filter tests.

projectName.description: EDT project name (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted).

tests.description: Comma-separated test names in Module.Method format.

timeout.description: Polling window in seconds (default: 60); on expiry returns Pending.

updateBeforeLaunch.description: Auto-chain (default: true): terminate a live client and run a silent DB update first.

name: run_yaxunit_tests

description: Literal/regex full-text search across all BSL modules in a project. Matching is purely textual and NOT ru/en dialect-aware, so a query in one BSL language won't find the other spelling; for identifier lookup use get_symbol_info, find_references or get_method_call_hierarchy instead. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('search_in_code').

contextLines.description: Lines of context before/after each match. Default: 2, max: 5

fileMask.description: Filter by module path substring (e.g. 'CommonModules' or 'Documents/SalesOrder')

isRegex.description: Treat query as a regular expression. Default: false

limit.description: Max matches returned with context. Default: 100, max: 500

metadataType.description: Filter by metadata type (e.g. 'documents', 'catalogs', 'commonModules'); more precise than fileMask. See guide for the full list.

outputMode.description: Output mode: 'full' (matches with context, default), 'count', or 'files'

query.description: Search string or regex pattern (required); matched literally unless isRegex=true

name: search_in_code

description: Set a line breakpoint on a 1C BSL module. Accepts either an EDT module-relative path (e.g. 'CommonModules/Foo/Module.bsl') or an absolute filesystem path. Use wait_for_break afterwards to block until the breakpoint is hit.

modulePath.description: Module identifier — EDT module path (CommonModules/Foo/Module.bsl) or absolute file path (required)

projectName.description: EDT project name (required when modulePath is module-relative)

name: set_breakpoint

breakpointId.description: Eclipse marker id of the created breakpoint (-1 if none)

degraded.description: True when only a marker-only breakpoint could be created

lineNumber.description: 1-based line number where the breakpoint was set

module.description: Legacy alias echo of the module identifier

modulePath.description: Module identifier as supplied (EDT path or absolute path)

resolvedFile.description: Workspace-relative path of the resolved .bsl file

warning.description: Warning text when the breakpoint is degraded/marker-only

description: Start performance measurement (замер производительности) on the active debug target. Enables line-level profiling: call counts and timing for every executed BSL line. Start-only and idempotent: if profiling is already active for this applicationId it stays on. Call stop_profiling to stop, then get_profiling_results to see which code was covered. Requires an active debug session (debug_launch or debug_yaxunit_tests).

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (required)

name: start_profiling

active.description: Whether profiling is now active for this applicationId

applicationId.description: Application id of the profiled debug session

started.description: True if this call started profiling, false if already active

description: Step a suspended debug thread. kind ∈ {over, into, out}. Blocks until the next SUSPEND event (or timeout) and returns the new frame snapshot.
name: step

applicationId.description: Application id of the debug session

frames.description: Stack frames: frameIndex, frameRef, name, line

hit.description: True if a suspend event was caught, false on timeout

topFrameRef.description: Stable ref of the top stack frame

description: Stop performance measurement (замер производительности) on the active debug target. Counterpart to start_profiling: deterministically switches profiling off. Idempotent: if profiling is not active for this applicationId it returns a benign result, not an error. Call get_profiling_results afterwards to retrieve the collected coverage.

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (required)

name: stop_profiling

active.description: Whether profiling is still active after the call

applicationId.description: Application id the result refers to

stopped.description: Whether profiling was actually toggled off

description: Terminate one or more 1С launches started from THIS EDT instance; externally launched 1С clients are never touched. Select ONE target mode: launchConfigurationName, projectName+applicationId, or all=true (needs confirm=true). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('terminate_launch').

all.description: Terminate every live EDT launch (optionally narrowed by projectName); requires confirm=true. Default false.

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications; requires projectName.

confirm.description: Required (true) when all=true; guards against accidental mass termination.

force.description: On polite-termination timeout, escalate to an OS-level process kill; may lose unsaved 1С state. Default false. Ignored for Attach.

includeAttach.description: Whether to act on Attach configs (disconnected, server keeps running). Default true; set false to skip them.

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact launch configuration name from list_configurations (single-launch mode).

projectName.description: EDT project name; pair with applicationId for one launch, or with all=true.

timeoutSeconds.description: Polite-wait window per launch, clamped to [1, 120]. Default from EDT preferences (factory default 10).

name: terminate_launch

description: Run EDT 'Translate configuration' on a configuration project - reads the dictionaries from the storages bound to it (external dictionary storage projects with the dependentProjectNature, or in-configuration storages) and regenerates the translated artifacts. Equivalent of the context-menu action Translation -> Translate configuration. Requires LanguageTool installed in EDT.

projectName.description: Project name (typically the source, e.g. the ru project). Required.

targetLanguages.description: Target language codes to synchronize (e.g. ["en"]). Required.

name: translate_configuration

description: Apply configuration changes to an application's database (infobase), full or incremental. Target by launchConfigurationName (preferred) or projectName + applicationId. Destructive/irreversible: run only on explicit request, and terminate any running 1C client on the target infobase first (exclusive lock). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('update_database').

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications; required if launchConfigurationName is omitted.

fullUpdate.description: true = full reload, false = incremental (default false).

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact runtime-client config name from list_configurations (preferred target).

projectName.description: EDT project name; required if launchConfigurationName is omitted.

name: update_database

applicationName.description: Display name of the target application.

message.description: Human-readable status message for the update.

stateAfter.description: Application update state after the update.

stateBefore.description: Application update state before the update.

updateType.description: Update mode applied: FULL or INCREMENTAL.

description: Validate 1C:Enterprise query language (QL) text against a project, returning syntax and semantic errors with line numbers. Use to check a query before embedding it in BSL; resolves table/field names against the project's metadata. Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('validate_query').

dcsMode.description: true for Data Composition System queries (allows DCS-specific syntax). Default: false.

queryText.description: Full 1C query text to validate (required), e.g. 'SELECT Ref FROM Catalog.Products'.

name: validate_query

dcsMode.description: Whether DCS validation mode was used

issues.description: Validation issues; each has severity, message, optional line/column/offset

valid.description: true when the query has zero issues

description: Wait for a debug suspend event (e.g. breakpoint hit) on the given application. Returns the suspended thread/frame snapshot, or {hit:false} on timeout. applicationId may be real or synthetic 'attach:<configName>'. If omitted and exactly one EDT debug launch is active, that launch is used. Does NOT terminate the launch on timeout — call again to keep waiting.

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (real or 'attach:<configName>'). Optional if exactly one debug launch is active.

name: wait_for_break

applicationId.description: Application id of the debug session waited on

frames.description: Stack frames of the suspended thread

hit.description: True if a suspend was observed, false on timeout

topFrameRef.description: Stable frame reference of the top stack frame

description: Write BSL source code to a 1C metadata object module. Use to edit a module: searchReplace a fragment (default, needs oldSource), replace the whole file, or append. Target the module by EITHER modulePath OR objectName (mutually exclusive — pass exactly one). Runs a BSL syntax check before writing (skipSyntaxCheck=true to force). Full parameters and examples: call get_tool_guide('write_module_source').

expectedHash.description: Lost-update guard for any mode: the contentHash from your last read; mismatch rejects.

expectedSource.description: Lost-update guard for mode=replace: the module content you last read; mismatch rejects.

modulePath.description: src/-relative module path, e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl'. Alternative to objectName (pass exactly one).

moduleType.description: Module type for objectName resolution (default ObjectModule).

objectName.description: Object name 'Type.Name', e.g. 'Document.MyDoc'. Resolved with moduleType. Alternative to modulePath.

oldSource.description: Fragment to find and replace; required for searchReplace, must match exactly once.

overwrite.description: Force mode=replace over an existing module without an expectedSource check (default false).

skipSyntaxCheck.description: Skip the BSL syntax check (default false).

source.description: BSL source to write (required): full file for replace, new fragment for searchReplace, text to add for append.

name: write_module_source
